title: Trichipteris borinquena
taxonomy: Trichipteris borinquena is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants, such as trees, for support. Trichipteris borinquena is native to the island of Puerto Rico, where it can be found in humid, tropical forests. The genus Trichipteris is part of the larger order Polypodiales, which includes a variety of ferns with multiple frond arrangements. As part of the class Polypodiopsida, Trichipteris borinquena is a member of a group of ferns that are thought to have evolved over 300 million years ago.
uses: Trichipteris borinquena, a species of fern found in the Caribbean, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal properties are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
